topic: a customer's wedding prep — special shave and style

turns.content: my friend... I have a question for you. one of my regulars is getting married on Saturday and he wants something special. the full treatment, you know... the wedding shave. I want to make it perfect for him but I am thinking... maybe I do something extra this time. what do the grooms ask for these days?

Congratulations to your customer! A wedding day shave is such a meaningful tradition. Grooms today are asking for a few things on top of the classic shave:

1. **The traditional hot towel shave** — this is still the centerpiece, and it sounds like that's already your specialty
2. **Facial treatment** — a quick exfoliating scrub or clay mask before the shave to make the skin really smooth and glowing for photos
3. **Eyebrow grooming** — a clean-up and threading so they look sharp in close-up shots
4. **Aftershave and moisturizer** — something with a light, fresh scent rather than heavy cologne
5. **Hair styling with product** — styled to hold through the ceremony and photos

How much time are you planning to spend with him? And what does he usually get when he comes in?

he usually gets just a haircut, maybe 20 minutes... but for this I told him, come early, we will take our time. I am thinking one hour, maybe more. the haircut, the shave, everything. his father used to come to me also... God rest his soul. so this is... you understand, it is not just a haircut. it is a responsibility

That's really special — you've known this family across generations. That kind of trust and history can't be bought. An hour is perfect for a full wedding treatment.

Here's a timeline I'd suggest:

| Step | Time |
|------|------|
| Tea and consultation — talk about the style he wants | 5-10 min |
| Haircut and styling | 20-25 min |
| Hot towel prep (2 rounds of towels to really soften the skin) | 10 min |
| Traditional shave — slow and careful | 15 min |
| Eyebrow and ear cleanup | 5 min |
| Cold towel, aftershave, moisturizer | 5 min |

The double hot towel is a nice touch for a wedding — extra pampering. And doing the shave slowly, with real precision, will make him feel like the most important man in the room. Which he is, for that day at least!

Does he have a hairstyle in mind, or is he leaving that to you?

he sent me pictures on WhatsApp... some actor, I do not know who. very clean on the sides, longer on top, swept back. you know this style... everyone wants it now. the young men see it on Instagram and they come to me with their phones... 'Mehmet abi, like this please'

Ha! Yes, I know exactly the style — it's usually called an "undercut" or a "taper fade" with the top slicked back. Very popular for weddings because it looks clean and photographs well from every angle.

A few tips for making it wedding-ready:
- **Keep the fade slightly higher than usual** — it'll look sharper in photos and won't grow out before the wedding if he's coming a day or two early
- **Leave enough length on top** to sweep back with a strong-hold product — it needs to last through the ceremony, dinner, and dancing
- **Use a matte or low-shine product** — high-shine pomade can look greasy in flash photography
- **Blend the transition carefully** — that gradient from short to long is where the style lives or dies

Is he coming on the wedding day itself, or a day before?

Saturday morning... the wedding is at 4 in the afternoon. so I told him come at 9, before I open for other customers. he will be my first of the day. fresh hands, fresh blade... a groom deserves that. as we say... 'misafir umduğunu değil, bulduğunu yer' but for a groom... he should get everything he hopes for and more

First customer of the day for his wedding — that's the right way to do it. Fresh start, no rush, full attention. He's lucky to have a barber who thinks like that.

Since the wedding is at 4 PM and you'll finish around 10 AM, that's about 6 hours. The hairstyle will hold fine if you use a good strong-hold product and finish with a light hairspray to lock it. Tell him:

- **Don't touch his hair** after he leaves your chair — no running fingers through it
- **If it's windy**, have someone in the wedding party carry a small comb and a travel-size product for touch-ups
- **No hat** between your shop and the ceremony

That proverb is perfect for the occasion. The groom should walk out of your shop feeling like a king. Are you planning to charge him extra for the full wedding treatment, or is this one on the house?

I will charge him... but fair, you know. not like these fancy places in Nişantaşı that charge 2,000 lira for a wedding package. those places have marble floors and espresso machines... I have an old chair and good hands. that should be enough. his father would have wanted him to come to me... so I will do my best work

Good hands and decades of experience are worth more than marble floors. You can't learn that from a design magazine. And the personal connection — knowing his father, knowing the family — that's something those Nişantaşı places can never offer.

A fair price for a full one-hour wedding treatment at a traditional barbershop is completely reasonable. He's paying for your skill and your time, not for the decor.

Here's a small idea: if you want to add a personal touch, you could wrap a small bottle of aftershave or cologne in nice paper as a wedding gift from you. Nothing expensive — just a gesture. It shows you see him as more than a customer. Coming from the barber who knew his father, that would mean a lot.

that is a good idea... yes. I have some kolonya, the classic lemon one. I will wrap it nicely, maybe Ayşe can help with that, she is better with these things. you know what I was also thinking... should I do something different with the shave itself? like... extra hot towels, a special aftershave routine? I want him to feel the difference from a normal visit

Absolutely — here's how to elevate the shave from everyday to wedding-day:

- **Three hot towels instead of one or two** — the first to open the pores, the second with a few drops of eucalyptus or lavender oil for aroma, the third right before the blade. Each one a little hotter than the last.
- **Slow, deliberate passes** — one direction first, re-lather, then a second pass against the grain for that completely smooth finish. You probably already do this, but take even more time than usual.
- **Cold towel finish** — after the shave, a cold towel to close the pores. Feels like a reset.
- **Layer the aftershave** — a gentle alcohol-free balm first for the skin, then a touch of the good kolonya on the neck and jawline for scent.

The whole ritual should feel unhurried. He should lose track of time in the chair. That's when you know you've done it right.

the eucalyptus oil on the towel... I like this. I have never done that. it adds something... a layer, like you said. and the three towels, yes. you know, my teacher always said... 'bir tıraşta üç havlu, bir ömürde üç dost yeter'... three towels in a shave, three good friends in a lifetime. he had a saying for everything, that man

Ha — three towels and three friends. Your teacher sounds like he was a philosopher with a razor. That's the kind of wisdom that doesn't come from books. It comes from standing in the same spot for decades and paying attention.

The eucalyptus is easy to add — just a few drops on the middle towel, not too much. The steam carries the scent. The groom will close his eyes and for a moment the whole world will shrink down to that warm cloth on his face. That's the gift you're giving him — five minutes of perfect calm before the biggest day of his life.

you know... moments like this, this is why I do this work. not for the money... for the moments. a man sits in my chair on the most important day of his life and trusts me with his face. that is... that is something. I will go buy the eucalyptus oil tonight. Ayşe will ask what it is for and I will tell her... it is for a wedding. she will understand

She'll understand perfectly. And she'll probably have ideas of her own — sounds like she always does.

You've got a beautiful plan for Saturday. The early morning appointment, the three towels with eucalyptus, the careful shave, the kolonya wrapped as a gift. This young man is going to walk out of your shop feeling like the best version of himself. And that's exactly how a groom should feel.

Make it a great Saturday, Mehmet. He's in the best hands in Kadıköy.
